hero.heading: Dùthchas Arainn
hero.content: A community-owned approach to sustainable land management on Arran

sections.heading: Dùthchas Arainn is a Community Benefit Society established to hold land and assets in community ownership for the benefit of Arran's people. We're working to create a sustainable future for our island through woodland crofting, natural conservation, and cultural heritage preservation.
Woodland Crofting in Arran for Biodiversity
Become a Member
content: Society membership is always open. Join 51 members who are already part of Dùthchas Arainn and have your say in how we manage land on Arran for generations to come.
Membership requires a minimum of 10 shares (£10). As a member you'll receive updates on the CAT application, invitations to AGMs and community events, and a vote in how the society is run.

sections.heading: Glenrickard Share Offer - Open Until 1st May 2026
content: Members can also invest in the Glenrickard Share Offer to fund a comprehensive feasibility study for woodland crofting in Forestry and Land Scotland's Glenrickard Land Management Area (Glencloy).

This Community Shares Standard Mark approved offer will support ecological assessments, business planning, and community consultation as we work toward Community Asset Transfer of this important woodland.
Investment:
• Minimum: £50 (50 shares @ £1 each)
• Target: £32,000
• Maximum target: £40,000
Timeline: Extended to 1st May 2026 (Beltane)
Progress: 1100 shares sold from 15 members (£1100 raised)

sections.heading: Woodland Crofting on Arran
content: Woodland crofting combines sustainable forestry with small-scale agriculture and affordable housing. Our Glenrickard pilot project will demonstrate how this model can work in Arran, supporting livelihoods while regenerating native woodland and protecting our island's cultural heritage.

cta.text: FIND OUT MORE ABOUT WOODLAND CROFTING

sections.heading: We aim to (amongst other aims!)
content: Secure by community asset transfer, and conserve, expand and connect areas of remaining ancient woodland for conservation, community access and regenerative land management, outdoor activities and education
Maintain and improve waterways within these woodland both for research, community enjoyment and conservation, but also flood mitigation purposes for our village communities at risk of coastal erosion
To investigate further, and preserve and protect, our cultural monuments throughout this highly rich and under-researched landscape

To develop low impact, sustainable and environmentally friendly 'green' homes for primary sector workers on the island, with an emphasis on affordability, using local materials and vernacular Arran design

sections.heading: What is Community Asset Transfer (CATS)?
content: Community Asset Transfers allow community organisations to acquire publicly-owned land at below-market value for community benefit. Established by the Community Empowerment (Scotland) Act 2015, this gives communities a legal right to request transfer of public assets from bodies like Forestry and Land Scotland.

Forestry and Land Scotland has completed 26 successful transfers since 2016, supporting projects from renewable energy to woodland management. For our project, CATS provides a proven pathway to community ownership, enabling sustainable woodland management that combines conservation with livelihood creation.

"Dùthchas" is a Gaelic word that encompasses a deep connection to land, heritage, and community—embodying our relationship with Arran and our responsibility to care for it. Learn more about dùthchas in this article: Dùthchas - What are we actually talking about?

sections.heading: Society Rules
content: Our full society rules are available below. Please note that the society was originally registered under the name "The Glen Community Benefit Society" and was later renamed to "Dùthchas Arainn Community Benefit Society." Both documents refer to the same organisation.

sections.heading: Second AGM - 25th October 2025
content: Our second AGM took place on Saturday 25th October 2025 at the Banner Lodge (Heather Lodge) in Brodick, where we launched the Glenrickard share offer.
